title: CRUD Operations with Mongoose
description: Perform Create, Read, Update, Delete operations using Mongoose models.

slides.title: Create – Adding Documents ✨
slides.content:
# Create

Two main ways:

1. `new Model(doc)` + `save()`
   ```javascript
   const user = new User({ name: 'Bob', email: 'bob@example.com' });
   await user.save();
   ```

2. `Model.create(doc)`
   ```javascript
   const user = await User.create({ name: 'Bob', email: 'bob@example.com' });
   ```

Both return the saved document.

slides.title: Read – Querying Documents 🔍
slides.content:
## Read Operations

- `Model.find(filter)` – returns array of matching documents.
- `Model.findOne(filter)` – returns the first match or `null`.
- `Model.findById(id)` – shortcut for `findOne({ _id: id })`.

Examples:
```javascript
const users = await User.find({ age: { $gte: 18 } }); // age >= 18
const user = await User.findOne({ email: 'alice@example.com' });
const userById = await User.findById('5f8d0d55b5b9c6a1c8b4567');
```

Mongoose supports MongoDB query operators like `$gt`, `$lt`, `$in`, etc.

slides.title: Update – Modifying Documents ✏️
slides.content:
## Update Operations

- `Model.updateOne(filter, update)` – updates first matching document.
- `Model.updateMany(filter, update)` – updates all matches.
- `Model.findByIdAndUpdate(id, update, options)` – finds by id and updates.

Options like `{ new: true }` return the updated document.

```javascript
await User.updateOne({ email: 'bob@example.com' }, { age: 31 });
const updated = await User.findByIdAndUpdate(id, { age: 32 }, { new: true });
```

slides.title: Delete – Removing Documents ❌
slides.content:
## Delete Operations

- `Model.deleteOne(filter)` – deletes first matching document.
- `Model.deleteMany(filter)` – deletes all matches.
- `Model.findByIdAndDelete(id)` – finds by id and deletes.

```javascript
await User.deleteOne({ email: 'bob@example.com' });
await User.findByIdAndDelete('5f8d0d55b5b9c6a1c8b4567');
```

slides.title: CRUD Example

quizData.question: Which Mongoose method finds a single document by its `_id`?
quizData.explanation: `Model.findById(id)` is a convenience wrapper for `findOne({ _id: id })`.
